agentWorkflow.task: Create the branch, assign abilities, keep this packet current, and create one PR for the hero.
Browse current ability sources for each backlog ability and record mechanics, delays, radius, target type, and source URLs.
Choose detector type, evidence priority, confidence, gameloop windows, and docstring/evidence description.
Add versioned rules and detector support in data/replay modules without changing unrelated heroes.
Parse at least two real replays for this hero and record generated skillshot stats in the PR body.
Check plausibility, double counting, evidence strength, tests, and PR summary completeness.

implementedRules.reason: A versioned skillshot landing rule already exists for this ability.

rule.evidenceDescription: Counts the second-heroic Deadly Charge execution with the same charge-line geometry used for the primary Deadly Charge rule.

implementedRules.reason: A versioned skillshot landing rule already exists for this ability.

rule.evidenceDescription: Counts Counter-Strike as landed when enemy Hero positions overlap the targeted triangular shockwave after the one-second channel. This is a geometry proxy: replays do not expose the enemy-Hero damage trigger directly in the current parser path.

implementedRules.reason: A versioned skillshot landing rule already exists for this ability.

rule.evidenceDescription: Counts the second-heroic Counter-Strike with the same targeted triangular shockwave proxy used for the primary Counter-Strike rule.

implementedRules.reason: A versioned skillshot landing rule already exists for this ability.

rule.evidenceDescription: Counts Discord Strike as landed when one or more enemy Hero units overlap a calibrated triangular area in front of Alarak after the 0.625 second cast delay. The replay target point supplies direction; the wider replay triangle compensates for one-second position samples.

implementedRules.reason: A versioned skillshot landing rule already exists for this ability.

rule.evidenceDescription: Counts Deadly Charge as landed when one or more enemy Hero units overlap Alarak's charge line. The release target point supplies direction, and the configured missile speed estimates where each enemy was when the charge reached their path distance.
